# Combined Index Across 10 Core CS Books (Deep, Difficulty-Ordered)

Auto-generated from all 10 listed books plus optional **Make Electronics**.

Each topic section opens with a per-book **summary table** showing what page range covers it and what the book teaches there. Below the summary, entries are grouped into **concept blocks** (e.g. AND gate, OR gate, Flip-flop). Inside each concept block, rows are sorted by **ascending difficulty**, with practical/hands-on rows interleaved alongside the theory rows of the same difficulty. Back-of-book index keywords for every book are collected in the appendix at the end of the file.

## Legend

- **Book** - short code; full title list below.
- **Book Pg** - printed page number in the book.
- **PDF Pg** - PDF reader page (offset from the book page by the front matter; useful to jump in your PDF viewer).
- **Diff** - difficulty 1 (gentle intro) -> 5 (deep specialist).
- **Lvl** - structural level: `Ch` = chapter, `Sec` = section, `Sub` = subsection, `Sbt` = subtopic, `Idx` = back-of-book index entry.
- **Exer** - if a chapter has an Exercises/Problems section, its starting book page is shown.
- **Kind** - `Theory` for narrative reading material, `Practical` for hands-on experiments, projects, labs, or chapter-end exercises.

## Books

| Code | Title | Difficulty tier |
| --- | --- | --- |
| MakeElec | Make: Electronics (Charles Platt) - optional, practical | 1 |
| Code | Code: The Hidden Language of Computer Hardware and Software (Petzold, 2nd ed.) | 1 |
| Justice | How Computers Really Work (Matthew Justice) | 1 |
| DDCA | Digital Design and Computer Architecture (Harris) | 2 |
| COD6 | Computer Organization And Design, 6th Edition (Patterson/Hennessy) | 3 |
| ModArch | Modern Computer Architecture | 4 |
| CSAPP | Computer Systems: A Programmer's Perspective, 3rd Edition (Bryant/O'Hallaron) | 4 |
| OSTEP | Operating Systems: Three Easy Pieces (Arpaci-Dusseau) | 2 |
| HLW | How Linux Works (Brian Ward) | 2 |
| LKD | Linux Kernel Development (Robert Love) | 5 |
| Kurose | Computer Networking: A Top-Down Approach (Kurose/Ross) | 2 |
